purpose: This policy describes how <<LAB_NAME>> selects, qualifies, and uses blood bank reagents (antisera, reagent red cells, antiglobulin reagents, controls). Required by 42 CFR 493.1252 (test systems), 42 CFR 493.1253 (performance verification), and 42 CFR 493.1271/1273 (immunohematology and QC).

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Immunohematology (control procedures)
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: If the laboratory performs ABO grouping, D(Rho) typing, unexpected antibody detection, antibody identification, or compatibility testing, the laboratory must, at a minimum, do the following: (1) Test reagent red cells to confirm reactivity. (2) Test antiserum (anti-A, anti-B, anti-A,B, anti-D) at the manufacturer's recommended concentration with reference red cells. (3) Test antiglobulin reagent (anti-IgG, polyspecific) for reactivity with IgG-sensitized red cells. (4) Test the negative control.

policy_statements: Blood bank reagents are FDA-cleared and used per the manufacturer's instructions; modified or non-cleared reagents are not used without performance establishment per the Method Verification policy and medical director or designee approval.

Reactivity QC on reagents is performed each day of use per 42 CFR 493.1273(a): reagent red cells confirmed reactive, antisera confirmed reactive with known antigen-positive reference cells, antiglobulin reagent confirmed reactive with IgG-sensitized reference cells, negative controls confirmed non-reactive.

Reagent lot acceptance: new lots are parallel-tested against the prior lot and against patient samples covering the expected range of reactivity; acceptance is documented before placing the new lot into service.
Storage of reagents per manufacturer instructions; expired reagents are removed from inventory and discarded.
Reagent QC records and lot acceptance documentation are retained for at least 10 years per 42 CFR 493.1105(a)(7).
procedure_steps: On reagent receipt, log lot, expiration, date, and store per manufacturer instructions.
Each day of use, perform reactivity QC per 42 CFR 493.1273(a); failed QC pauses patient testing on the affected reagent.
On new-lot introduction, perform parallel testing against the prior lot using reference cells and patient samples; document acceptance.
Discard expired reagents and log the disposal.
definitions: Daily-of-use quality control on blood bank reagents confirming each reagent performs as expected with known reference cells.
Reagent containing antibodies to human immunoglobulin used to detect antibody bound to red cells. Polyspecific (anti-IgG plus anti-complement) or monospecific (anti-IgG only).
